title: Streams, Redirection, Quoting, and Pipelines · Command Retrieval
summary: Build a repeatable incident-report pipeline whose data and diagnostics remain distinct and whose failures cannot be hidden by downstream success. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a stream-routing and pipeline-trace workbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace standard input, standard output, standard error, file descriptors, and terminal attachment across a command.
Predict truncating, appending, input, descriptor duplication, and combined redirection from left-to-right order.
Apply unquoted, single-quoted, double-quoted, escaped, and ANSI-C quoted contexts to preserve intended arguments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Stdin Stdout Stderr · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — A 8-record incident command writes selected rows to report.txt and diagnostics to report.err. One grep stage finds no matches; predict descriptor routing and final status with pipefail before running. Competency probe: Trace standard input, standard output, standard error, file descriptors, and terminal attachment across a command.

Which action demonstrates Stdin Stdout Stderr without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace standard input, standard output, standard error, file descriptors, and terminal attachment across a command.

content.text: A 8-record incident command writes selected rows to report.txt and diagnostics to report.err. One grep stage finds no matches; predict descriptor routing and final status with pipefail before running. content.title: Commit before feedback

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: All command output travels through one stream.

All command output travels through one stream. This does not resolve stdin stdout stderr · changed case.

This does not satisfy Stdin Stdout Stderr for a stream-routing and pipeline-trace workbook.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use command substitution and grouping without losing newlines, status, scope, or observable failure evidence.
